content.text: The system shall provide access to the Rules and Action page under RAMS → Rack → IRDS → Rules and Action.
access the Rules and Action page from the navigation path RAMS → Rack → IRDS → Rules and Action,
I can manage rack elements and associated actions.
the user navigates through the path RAMS → Rack → IRDS → Rules and Action,
the user clicks the “Rules and Action” menu,
the system shall open the Rules and Action page successfully.
The system shall display a searchable and sortable list of rack elements with columns: Element PIN, Element Name, Category, Status, and Action.
the Rules and Action page to display a table of rack elements,
I can view all existing elements and their details.
the Rules and Action page is loaded,
it shall display a sortable and searchable table with columns:
The system shall allow users to search elements using the Search bar at the top of the page, filtering results dynamically as text is entered.
search for specific rack elements using a search bar,
I can quickly find the elements I need.
the table of rack elements is displayed,
the user types text in the search bar,
the results shall dynamically filter and update based on matching text in any column.
The system shall provide a dropdown filter (e.g., “RAMS Recommends”) allowing users to view customized or predefined rule sets.
I can view customized or predefined rule sets for rack elements.
the user opens the dropdown filter,
a predefined rule set (like “RAMS Recommends”) is selected,
the table shall refresh and display only the elements applicable to that rule set.
The system shall include a Risk Assessment Chart button that redirects to or displays risk evaluation charts related to rack elements.
I can quickly view graphical risk evaluation data for rack elements.
the user clicks the Risk Assessment Chart button,
it shall open or redirect to a page displaying risk evaluation charts.
The system shall provide an “Add New Element” button at the top right corner of the page to add a new element entry.
an Add New Element button on the Rules and Action page,
I can create new rack element entries easily.
the user is on the Rules and Action page,
the user clicks Add New Element,
a modal popup shall appear with:
On clicking Add New Element, a modal popup shall appear with mandatory input fields labelled Element Name, Reference Element dropdown, Geometry Type dropdown and action buttons Save and Cancel.
Reference Element shall include all standard Element.
a modal popup to appear when I click “Add New Element”
I can enter the name of a new element and save it.
a modal popup shall appear containing input fields labelled “Element Name” and “Reference Element” and “Geometry Type” dropdown along with Save and Cancel buttons.
The Geometry type dropdown shall include the following options – Node, Line and Plate.
the Geometry Type dropdown to show predefined geometry options
I can select the correct geometry type while configuring the element.
the Geometry Type dropdown is visible on the form,
the user clicks to expand the dropdown,
the system shall display the following selectable options: Node, Line, and Plat.
The system shall validate that the Element Name field is not empty before enabling the Save button.
the system to validate that the Element Name field is not empty,
the Add Element modal is open,
the Element Name field is empty,
the Save button shall remain disabled.
the Save button shall become enabled.
If the user clicks Save, the new element shall be added to the table with a unique Element PIN automatically generated by the system.
it gets added to the system with a unique PIN.
the user enters a valid Element Name and clicks Save,
Generate a unique Element PIN automatically
Add the element to the table.
The system shall assign a default Category as “General” when adding a new element manually.
the system to assign a default Category automatically when adding an element,
the user adds a new element,
the system shall auto-assign a default Category (General) when the user adds a new element manually.
The system shall allow each element’s Status to be toggled between Active and Inactive using a switch button.
toggle the status of each element between Active and Inactive,
I can control which elements are currently in use.
the user toggles the status switch,
the element’s status shall change immediately between Active (blue) and Inactive (grey).
The system shall display the current status in text format beside the toggle: “Active” (blue) or “Inactive” (grey).
the current status text to be displayed beside the toggle,
I can visually identify an element’s current state.
the text “Active” shall be shown in blue.
the text “Inactive” shall be shown in grey.

The system shall allow users to perform additional actions for each element via the three-dot (⋮) menu in the Action column (e.g., Edit Element, Rack Health Parameter, Edit Activity Phase, Pre-Inspection Guide).
use the three-dot (⋮) menu beside each element,
I can perform additional actions such as Edit, Delete, or View Details.
the user clicks the three-dot (⋮) menu in the Action column,
the options Edit Element, Rack Health Parameter, Edit New Activity Phase and Pre-Inspection Guide.
On clicking Cancel in the Add Element popup, the input field shall be cleared, and focus shall return to the Element Name field.
cancel the input field in the Add Element modal,
I can clear the field and start over.
the Add Element modal is open,
the Element Name field shall be cleared,
the cursor focus shall return to that field.
The system shall show pagination controls at the bottom of the table, allowing navigation between pages and adjusting rows per page (e.g., 10, 25, 50).
I can navigate between multiple pages of rack elements.
the element list contains more rows than the current page limit,
the user scrolls to the bottom,
pagination controls shall appear showing navigation options and a “Rows per Page” selector (10, 25, 50).
The system shall update the table view immediately after a new element is added, without requiring a page reload.
the table to update instantly after adding or editing an element,
I don’t need to reload the page to see changes.
the user adds, edits, or deletes an element,
the system shall refresh the table data automatically without a full page reload
